title: Query board by name

posts.author: Sonja (Sonja)
posts.cookedHtml:
How can I query board by its name? I need to see if a board with this name doesn’t exist to create with duplicate_boards. I’ve tried

query {

  boards (name: "my board name") {

      ids

    name

    state

    board_folder_id

    owner {

      id

    }

  }

}

but I’m getting

"errors": [
        {
            "message": "Field 'boards' doesn't accept argument 'name'",
            "locations": [
                {
                    "line": 3,
                    "column": 11
                }
            ],
            "fields": [
                "query",
                "boards",
                "name"
            ]
        },
        {
            "message": "Field 'ids' doesn't exist on type 'Board'",
            "locations": [
                {
                    "line": 5,
                    "column": 7
                }
            ],
            "fields": [
                "query",
                "boards",
                "ids"
            ]
        }
    ],

Please advice

posts.author: f2yez (Fayez Bdair)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I would like to double the request by Sonja. I am currently working on a scenario, where I want to search boards by name matching with keywords completely or partially, it will be awesome if you can release something like getBoardsByValue like itemsByValue query.

Thanks!

posts.author: TMNXT-Dev (Ilja Zweigert)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey guys,

quick walkthrough:

  1. query your boards:
query {
  boards{
    id name
  }
}
  1. You will get something like this:

  1. Filter your response.

  2. Done

posts.author: binod7 (Binod Chaudhary)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s only suitable for few data. If I had to search inside 1000+ boards, I need to fetch all of them first, which is not a feasible solution. Is there any update yet?

posts.author: cygnus (Ajit Singh)
posts.cookedHtml: Yeah it only works for a few boards if there are more than a thousand boards and documents you get both in boards api. You cannot even filter by type then atleast everything that a user can identify we should be able to filter on that.
